🏳️‍🌈 🏳️‍⚧️ 🌈

Papaya Pride Pub Quiz

Five rounds celebrating LGBTQ+ history, icons, culture & music. Good luck!

Round 1Pride History

Time to flex your LGBTQ+ history muscles. From landmark moments to legal milestones, how well do you know the story of Pride?

In 1969, LGBTQ+ bars in New York City were frequently raided by the police. One such raid sparked a famous uprising that many consider the beginning of the modern LGBTQ+ liberation movement. What was the name of the bar?

In 1984, a group of LGBTQ+ activists came together under the initials LGSM to raise money and support for striking workers. Which group of workers did they support?

Which country became the first in the world to legalise same-sex marriage?

In what year did the World Health Organization remove homosexuality from its list of diseases?

Polari was a secret language used by many LGBTQ+ people in Britain. Which of the following words did not originate from Polari?

Round 2Pop Culture Slang

A lot of the slang you hear on social media and from Gen Z today has its roots in LGBTQ+ communities - particularly Black and Latino ballroom culture. How fluent are you?

Match each slang phrase to its definition.

A. Tea
B. Ate
C. Mother
D. Shade
E. Kiki
Round 3Queer Icons

From the arts to politics, science to sport - LGBTQ+ people have shaped history in every field imaginable. How many of these icons can you name?

Which British mathematician and codebreaker helped crack the Enigma code during WWII?

Which Irish playwright and author wrote The Picture of Dorian Gray?

Which British comedian, writer and broadcaster hosted QI from 2003-2016?

Which tennis legend defeated Bobby Riggs in the 1973 Battle of the Sexes match?

Harvey Milk became one of the first openly gay elected officials in the US when elected in which city?

Round 4Pride on Screen

The following images are taken from TV shows and films featuring LGBTQ+ actors or characters. Some are classics, some are modern hits, and a couple might separate the quiz champions from the rest. Can you name them all?

Round 5Music Round

Time for some Pride bangers! Every artist in this round identifies as LGBTQ+. Name the artist for 1 point and the song title for another - 2 points per track, 20 points up for grabs. Good luck!
